title: E2E tests — wizard gating, progress monotonicity, write-only regression
description: Lock in the new wizard contract with browser tests. The headline tests are: (1) GET /my-profile/{token} on a fully-populated player still returns ZERO stored answer values in the HTML — the privacy regression test from Sprint 016 must continue to pass under the new tier-aware GET; (2) POSTing tier 2 with intent=continue advances a fresh user to tier 3 visibly; (3) POSTing tier 2 by a user at wizard_progress_tier=4 keeps progress at 4; (4) the 'Save & finish here' button on any tier renders the confirmation page and does NOT advance the user; (5) localStorage draft hydration round-trip; (6) backfill: a player seeded directly with tier-3 answers but wizard_progress_tier=0 gets bumped to 3 by the migration.
acceptance_criteria: Privacy regression test: GET on a fully-populated player returns no stored answer values in the rendered HTML (extends Sprint 016 my_tennis_privacy.spec.ts)
Wizard progression test: fresh user lands on tier 1; POST intent=continue advances to tier 2; repeat through tier 6
Terminal CTA test: clicking 'Save & finish here' on any tier renders confirmation, never the next tier
Monotonicity test: a user at progress=4 who POSTs tier=2 has wizard_progress_tier still =4 afterwards (verified via admin read view)
All-done state test: a user with wizard_progress_tier=6 sees the re-edit affordance, not a fresh tier 1
localStorage test: drafted fields in tier 3 survive a page reload; clearing happens after a successful POST
Backfill test: a SQL fixture seeds answers in tier 3 only, runs migration 027, asserts wizard_progress_tier=3
Existing Sprint 016 tests (write-only contract, merge semantics, captain-note leak regression) all still pass

files_to_create: tests/migrations/027_backfill_test.sql — fixture + assertions for backfill behaviour (or equivalent Go test)
files_to_modify: tests/e2e/fixtures/seed.sql — may need a player at varying wizard_progress_tier values for tests
key_decisions: Backfill correctness is best tested at the SQL/Go level rather than in the browser — it's deterministic on raw data
Browser tests focus on user-visible contracts (no answers in HTML, CTAs do what they say, progress is monotonic)

testing_requirements: Run via existing make test-e2e infrastructure
All Sprint 016 my_tennis specs continue to pass — no regressions

context.user_story: As the maintainer, I want the wizard's privacy and progression contracts to be encoded as automated tests so that future template or handler refactors can't silently leak stored answers or invert the consent flow.
design_principles: Privacy is enforced by tests, not just code review
Every named contract in the SUMMARY has at least one test
